page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a split performance profile. Single-core processing is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains average for both cached and uncached operations, though the PassMark memory score and database operations are classified as weak. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput both achieve average tier results.

Given the shared CPU allocation, lack of local storage, and moderate network baseline, this instance offers a cost-effective resource profile for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores or high-speed local disk I/O. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, development environments, and multi-threaded tasks that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling and moderate memory performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on a shared Intel Xeon processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Network performance is limited to a 1 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks reveal a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and database operations are weak, while multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput reach average tiers. Due to its shared CPU allocation and moderate performance profile, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for non-critical workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded utility processing, and static web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, offering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly general-purpose server offering shared Intel Xeon processing and balanced memory for non-critical multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average multi-core CPU and static web serving performance
Weak single-core processing and database operation benchmarks
Suited for general-purpose applications and development environments
